title: Flin Flon Reminder
description: The Flin Flon Reminder was founded as a daily newspaper on October 16, 1946, to serve the town of Flin Flon. Flin Flon crosses the border between Manitoba and Saskatchewan, with the majority of the town located within Manitoba. The newspaper reduced the frequency of its print editions to three days per week in April 2005 and to two days per week in August 2015. The community newspaper became a weekly publication on January 31, 2017.

title: Kelowna Capital News
description: Kelowna Capital News cut its Friday print edition on March 26, 2020, due to the impacts of COVID-19 and continued to publish a print edition on Wednesdays. The newspaper confirmed in April 2022 that the change to a weekly print edition had become permanent. The newspaper now publishes its print edition on Thursdays. Kelowna Capital News previously reduced its publication schedule to twice per week from three editions per week on September 15, 2014.

title: Lâ€™Ã‰cho de Victoriaville
description: Lâ€™Ã‰cho de Victoriaville, a Quebecor publication, competed with Transcontinental's La Nouvelle Union. It was launched in 2012 and closed in September 2014.

title: Golden Ears Daily
description: The Golden Ears Daily, a Black Press-owned free daily, ceased publication in November, 2013.

title: The Dunnville Chronicle
description: The Dunnville Chronicle and the West Niagara News were closed by Sun Media in November, 2012.

title: Victoria News Daily
description: The Victoria News Daily ceased publication in November 2013. (It is different from the Victoria News,  a community newspaper. )

title: La Nouvelle
description: La Nouvelle, a Transcontinental community paper, ceased publication in the first quarter of 2012.

title: Markdale Standard
description: Markdale Standard, a Sun Media community newspaper, ceased publication in the third quarter of 2012.

title: The Charter
description: In October 2013 Transcontinental  Media closed The Charter, a community paper that served Placentia for 16 years.

title: Amherst News
description:
SaltWire Network confirmed in March 2021 that Amherst News had permanently closed. The weekly community newspaper had been temporarily closed since March 25, 2020, due to the impacts of the pandemic.
The newspaper absorbed The Citizen Record on July 1, 2018 and changed from a paid-for community newspaper to a free community paper. The Amherst News stopped daily publication and became a weekly newspaper as of Aug. 2, 2013.

title: Lindsay Post
description: The Quebecor-owned Lindsay Post ceased publication in 2013. At the time of closure it appeared twice per week on Tuesdays and Fridays. Back in May 2007, when the paper was owned by Osprey Media, the paper changed its name from The Daily Post to The Lindsay Post to reflect its change from publishing Monday to Friday to Tuesdays and Fridays.

title: Oakville Beaver
description: On April 1, 2026, the Oakville Beaver began publishing a print edition again. The free monthly print editions are available to pick up from local businesses across the city. On September 15, 2023, Metroland announced that all of its 71 community newspapers would immediately shift to digital-only publications, including the Oakville Beaver. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists. In September 2014 the Oakville Beaver cancelled its Wednesday edition and only published print editions on Thursdays and Fridays. The newspaper was founded in 1981.

title: Metro London
description: Metro London, a free daily paper launched by Metro Canada on April 4, 2011, closed in late 2014.

title: Metro Regina
description: In April 2012, Metro Canada launched Metro Regina with a daily print edition, website and mobile apps. It closed in the third quarter of 2014.

title: Metro Saskatoon
description: In April 2012, Metro Canada launched Metro Saskatoon with a daily print edition, website and mobile apps. It closed in the third quarter of 2014.

title: Cape Breton Star
description: The  independently-owned Chronicle Herald launched the Cape Breton Star community newspaper in May 2014. The paper ceased publication in the fall of 2016.

title: Chronicle Herald
description: SaltWire Network stopped home delivery service for the Chronicle Herald in some rural communities in Nova Scotia on November 28, 2022. SaltWire Network announced on October 3 that its daily newspapers, including The Chronicle Herald, would be cutting their Monday print editions effective October 17, 2022. Digital versions of the newspapers will remain available online. Previously, The Chronicle Herald cancelled its Sunday edition on April 20, 2013, and offered an expanded weekend edition published on Saturdays.

title: Ottawa RushHour
description: Ottawa RushHour, a free daily paper launched by CanWest, closed in April 2008, after surviving for just over a year.

title: Calgary RushHour
description: Calgary RushHour, a free daily paper launched by CanWest, closed in April 2008, after surviving for just over a year.

title: Edmonton RushHour
description: Edmonton RushHour, a free daily paper launched by CanWest, closed in April 2008, after surviving for just over a year.

title: 24 Heures - Gatineau
description: Quebecor-owned 24 Heures Gatineau-Ottawa, a free daily newspaper, ceased publication on May 9, 2008.

title: Around the Block
description: Around the Block published its final issue in February 2024. The community newspaper was published every two months by the Human Development Council and was founded in October 2008. It featured articles written by local residents and community organizations, and served the five lowest-income neighbourhoods in Saint John, New Brunswick.

title: Jasper Booster
description: Jasper Booster, a Sun Media-owned community newspaper, ceased publishing in March 2009.

title: Morinville Redwater Town & Country Examiner
description: The Morinville Redwater Town and Country Examiner was born in November 2008 out of the merging of the Morinville Mirror and Redwater Tribune. The paper was shuttered several months later.

title: Nelson Daily News
description: The final print run of the Nelson Daily News was in July 2010. The paper was shut down a few weeks after Black Press purchased it from Glacier Media.

title: Barrhaven Independent
description: The Barrhaven Independent closed as part of a transition to launch a new community newspaper (Barrhaven Independent - Your Community Voice) that is part of the Your Community Voice chain of community newspapers in Ottawa. The last issue of the community newspaper was published on January 6, 2025. Previously, the newspaper was a weekly publication that moved to web-only publication in early 2012. It relaunched a print edition in February 2013, publishing twice per month. The newspaper had been serving the Barrhaven community in Ottawa since 1990.
Sources: ["Barrhaven Independent staff","Newspapers Canada Year in Review - 2012","https:\/\/barrhavenindependent.ca\/","https:\/\/issuu.com\/jmorris0","https:\/\/sites.google.com\/view\/your-community-voice\/manotick-barrhaven","https:\/\/drive.google.com\/file\/d\/1RqT0PjYHJZ_sKIdAtey3ysX5UF7PsOKT\/view"]

title: Whistler Today
description: Whistler today  was a local free daily that launched by Glacier Media in 2006. It was closed March 2008.

title: Saskatoon Express
description: Saskatoon Express, a weekly community newspaper covering hyperlocal news in Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, published its final edition on May 13, 2019. The newspaper launched on June 25, 2011.

title: LaSalle Silhouette

title: Tecumseh Tribune

title: Caledonia Regional News This Week
description: The Caledonia Regional News This Week closed at the beginning of 2012.

title: NewsNow
description: NewsNow announced on May 9, 2014, that the newspaper would be expanding its website to offer more online content. The weekly community newspaper launched on May 4, 2012, and serves the Grimsby, Lincoln, West Lincoln and Winona communities in Ontario.
Community: Grimsby, Lincoln, West Lincoln and Winona, Ontario

title: The Montreal Mirror
description: The Montreal Mirror, a community newspaper owned by Sun Media, was closed in June 2012.

title: Edmonton Journal
description: Postmedia announced on September 21 that nine daily newspapers, including Edmonton Journal, would be cutting their Monday print editions effective October 17, 2022. Digital versions of the newspapers will remain available online. The Edmonton Journal previously cancelled its Sunday edition in June 2012.

title: Ottawa Citizen
description: Postmedia announced on September 21 that nine daily newspapers, including Ottawa Citizen, would be cutting their Monday print editions effective October 17, 2022. Digital versions of the newspapers will remain available online.The Ottawa Citizen previously stopped publishing a Sunday edition in June 2012.

title: Gazeta (Toronto ed.)
description: In February 2019, Gazeta stopped publishing all print editions and shifted to become an online publication. The Polish-language newspaper launched in 1987 and published a print edition three times per week, but it became a weekly in September 2014.

title: Amherstburg Echo
description: The Amherstburg Echo, one of the oldest newspapers in Canada, closed in October 2012.

title: Capital City News
description: On March 14, 2012, Sun Media launched Ottawa Capital City News, a weekly newspaper. It closed it in December 2012.

title: West Niagara News
description: West Niagara News was closed by Sun Media in November 2012.

title: Guelph Review
description: On March 14, 2012, Sun Media launched the Guelph Review, a weekly newspaper. It closed at the end of 2012.

title: Kitchener-Waterloo Review (aka K-W Review)

title: Windsor This Week
description: On March 14, 2012, Sun Media launched Windsor This Week, a weekly newspaper in Windsor, Ont.  It closed by the end of the year.

title: Leamington Post
description: The Sun-owned Leamington Post, a fixture in the community for 138 years, closed at the end of 2012. It was a community newspaper.

title: The Graphic
description: Postmedia announced on June 26, 2018, that The Graphic weekly newspaper would stop publishing its print edition and become a digital only publication. In March 2013, the Portage Daily Graphic shifted from a daily newspaper to a weekly paper (publishing on Tuesdays) and was renamed The Graphic. The first issue of the Daily Graphic was published on July 2, 1895.

title: Town Crier - Bayview Mills
description: In May 2013 Multimedia Nova Corp. ceased publication of its Town Crier newspapers in the Greater Toronto Area after filing for bankruptcy. In August 2013 the Town Crier newspaper chain was purchased by a group of former employees with plans to re-launch the papers. This did not happen in the case of the Bayview Mills paper.

title: Two Row Times
description: Two Row Times is a free weekly newspaper that serves Indigenous communities in Ontario and is distributed to every First Nation reserve in Ontario. Two Row Times was launched in August 2013 soon after the Tekawennake News closed. The first issue was published on August 14, 2013.

title: Crowsnest Pass Promoter
description: The Crowsnest Pass Promoter was closed by Sun Media in August 2013.

title: Etobicoke Guardian
description: In September 2013, the Etobicoke Guardian cancelled its Tuesday edition and reduced service to one print edition per week on Thursdays. On September 15, 2023, Metroland announced that all of its 71 community newspapers would immediately shift to digital-only publications, including the Etobicoke Guardian. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists. The newspaper was founded in 1917.

title: North York Mirror
description: North York Mirror stopped publishing a Tuesday print edition in September 2013 and became a weekly paper. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists. The newspaper launched in 1957.

title: Scarborough Mirror
description: In September 2013, the Metroland-owned Scarborough Mirror community newspaper cancelled its Tuesday print edition and became a weekly paper. On September 15, 2023, Metroland announced that all of its 71 community newspapers would immediately shift to digital-only publications, including the Scarborough Mirror. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists. The newspaper launched in 1962.

title: Uxbridge Standard
description: The Uxbridge Standard ceased to exist after Skyline Media merged it and the Scugog Standard in January 2013 to create The Standard.

title: Scugog Standard
description: The Scugog Standard ceased to exist afer Skyline Media merged it and the Uxbridge Standard in January 2013 to create The Standard.

title: Abbotsford Times
description: Black Press closed the Abbotsford Times, a community newspaper, at the end of 2013.

title: Kamloops Daily News
description: Glacier Media Group closed the Kamloops Daily News on Jan. 11, 2014, leaving British Columbiaâ€™s 12th largest city without a daily newspaper.

title: Kamloops This Week
description:
Kamloops This Week published its final edition on October 25, 2023, resulting in the loss of 26 jobs. The community newspaper was the last newspaper serving Kamloops. The owner cited rising costs resulting from their printer closing as well as the loss of page views and ad revenue caused by Canadian news links being blocked by Meta and Google as reasons behind the closing. 
On April 1, 2020, Kamloops This Week stopped publishing a Friday edition due to the impacts of the pandemic, but continued to publish a print edition once per week on Wednesdays. In January 2014, in response to the impending closure of the Daily News, Thompson River Publications Partnership Ltd. (the previous owner) announced that Kamloops This Week would increase its publication dates to three days per week effective January 17, 2014.
The community newspaper published its first issue on August 28, 1988.

title: Peace Arch News Daily
description: Peace Arch News Daily, a Black Press title, ceased publication in March 2014. A community newspaper, called Peace Arch News, remains in circulation.

title: The Sarnia Journal
description: The Sarnia Journal, an independent community newspaper serving Sarnia, Ontario, was launched in March 2014 as a free weekly newspaper. In April 2020, the newspaper shifted from weekly to biweekly print editions due to the impacts of the pandemic. The newspaper also stopped offering free home delivery in November 2021 because of the reduction in advertising income that resulted from the pandemicâ€™s impact on local businesses. In April 2022, print editions increased to four editions per month. The community paper took a hiatus during the summer of 2022 and announced on September 7, 2022, that the newspaper would be relaunching as a digital only publication.

title: Nanaimo News Bulletin Daily
description: The Black Press free daily newspaper, Nanaimo News Bulletin Daily ceased publication March 2014. The community newspaper, Nanaimo News Bulletin, remains in publication.

title: Anishinabek News
description: Anishinabek News is a community newspaper that was founded in 1988 to cover local news for the Anishinabek Nation. The website was launched on April 16, 2013 to provide more timely news. After the March 2014 issue, the newspaper stopped publishing its monthly print edition. Anishinabek News began publishing a free quarterly print edition beginning with the Summer 2017 issue.

title: The Standard
description: The Uxbridge Standard and the Scugog Standard merged in January 2013 to create The Standard. The weekly community newspaper covers local news for the North Durham region in Ontario. The first issue of The Standard was published on January 10, 2013.

title: tonight
description: The free biweekly newspaper in Toronto published its final issue on May 29, 2014. The newspaper was sold to Annex Business Media in January, 2013, and the daily newspaper was reduced to publishing twice per week. The first edition was published on September 8, 2009.

title: The Grid
description: The Star Media Group's Toronto community newspaper The Grid published its final issue on July 3, 2014. The weekly newspaper launched on May 12, 2011.

title: Here Magazine

title: Xtra Vancouver

title: La Presse

description: The Fraser Valley Daily,  a free newspaper owned by Black Press that covered Chilliwack and Abbotsford, ceased publication in 2013.

title: Dawson Creek Daily News
description: In early 2014 Glacier Media merged two daily newspapers in the Peace River region of British Columbia: the Dawson Creek Daily News and the Alaska Highway News in Fort St. John. The company said the Alaska Highway News would expand to cover Dawson Creek, Fort Nelson to the north and the surrounding areas.

title: The Bridgewater Bulletin
description: The Bridgewater Bulletin ceased to exist in May 2011 when it merged with the Lunenburg Progress Enterprise and become The Lunenburg County Progress Bulletin.

title: Colborne Chronicle
description: In February 2009, Quebecor's Colborne Chronicle, a community paper, ceased to exist because it was merged with the daily Port Hope Evening Guide and the Cobourg Daily Star to create Northumberland Today.

title: Cobourg Daily Star
description: In February 2009, Quebecor's Cobourg Daily Star ceased to exist because it merged with the Port Hope Evening Guide, a daily, and the Colborne Chronicle, a community paper, to create Northumberland Today.

title: Port Hope Evening Guide
description: In February 2009, Quebecor daily The Port Hope Evening Guide ceased to exist because it merged with the Cobourg Daily Star and the Colborne Chronicle, a community paper, to create Northumberland Today.

title: Wainwright Star
description: The Wainrwright Star and the Wainwright Edge merged to create the Wainright Star/Edge, effective Nov. 1, 2013. The newsly created community paper appears on Fridays.

title: Wainwright Star/Edge
description: The Wainrwright Star and the Wainwright Edge merged to create the Wainright Star/Edge, effective Nov. 1, 2013. The newly created community paper appears on Fridays.

title: Wainwright Edge
description: The Wainrwright Star and the Wainwright Edge merged to create the Wainright Star/Edge, effective Nov. 1, 2013. The newly created  community paper appears on Fridays.

title: The Cariboo Advisor
description: The Black Press-owned The Cariboo Adviser ceased to exist because it merged with the Williams Lake Tribune and its weekend edition, Tribune Weekend, on Sept. 4, 2013. The newspapers were replaced by the free community paper, the Williams Lake Tribune, which is distributed on Wednesdays, and the Weekend Advisor, published on Fridays.

title: Williams Lake Tribune
description: The Black Press-owned paper The Cariboo Adviser, the Williams Lake Tribune and its weekend edition, Tribune Weekend, merged on Sept. 4, 2013. They were replaced by the free community paper, the Williams Lake Tribune, distributed on Wednesdays and the Weekend Advisor, published on Fridays.

title: Beaverlodge Advertiser
description: In January 14, 2014 the Beaverlodge Advertiser and the Beaverlodge West County News merged to create the Beaverlodge West County News & Advertiser.

title: Beaverlodge West County News
description: In January 14, 2014 the Beaverlodge Advertiser and the Beaverlodge West County News merged to create the Beaverlodge West County News & Advertiser.

title: Beaverlodge West County News & Advertiser
description: In January 14, 2014 the Beaverlodge Advertiser and the Beaverlodge West County News merged to create the Beaverlodge West County News & Advertiser.

title: Canmore Leader
description: Canmore Leader, owned by Sun Media, ceased to exist after it was merged with the Banff Crag & Canyon and became the Bow Valley Crag & Canyon in June 2013.

title: Banff Crag & Canyon
description: The Sun Media-owned Banff Crag & Canyon ceased to exist after it merged with the Canmore Leader and became the Bow Valley Crag & Canyon in June 2013.

title: Bow Valley Crag & Canyon
description: On February 27, 2023, Postmedia shifted 12 community newspapers in Alberta to digital only publications, including the Bow Valley Crag & Canyon. Canmore Leader, owned by Sun Media, merged with the Banff Crag & Canyon to become the Bow Valley Crag & Canyon in June 2013.
Community: Banff, Canmore and surrounding Bow Valley region, Alberta

title: The Daily News
description: The final print run of the the Prince Rupert Daily News was in July 2010. The paper was shut down a few weeks after Black Press purchased it fom Glacier Media.

title: The Lunenburg County Progress Bulletin
description: The Lunenburg County Progress Bulletin was created through the merger of the Bridgewater Bulletin (est. 1887) and Lunenburg Progress Enterprise (est. 1876) in May 2011. The new publication is a locally owned independent newspaper published every Wednesday by Lighthouse Media Group.

title: Valley Now
description: Transcontinental Media launched a new community newspaper in Nova Scotia on November 12. Valley Now is a free weekly publication serving the residents of Kings, Annapolis and Hants counties.

title: Arnprior Chronicle Guide
description: The Metroland-owned Arnprior Chronicle Guide ceased to exist after it was merged with the  Arnprior EMC  to create the Arnprior Chronicle EMC in October 2011.

title: Arnprior EMC
description: Metroland-owned Armprior EMC ceased to exist whe it was purchased by Metroland and merged with the Arnprior Chronicle Guide to create the Arnprior Chronicle EMC in October 2011.

title: Arnprior Chronicle-Guide
description: Metroland purchased Arnprior EMC and merged it with the Arnprior Chronicle Guide to create the Arnprior Chronicle Guide EMC in October 2011, later renamed Arnprior Chronicle-Guide. On September 15, 2023, Metroland announced that all of its 71 community newspapers would immediately shift to digital-only publications, including the Arnprior Chronicle-Guide. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists.

title: Kanata EMC
description: Kanata EMC ceased to exist after Metroland purchased the paper and merged it with the Kanata Kourier Standard to create the Kanata Kourier Standard EMC in October 2011.

title: Stayner Sun
description: Metroland community newspapers The Stayner Sun, established in 1877, and the Wasaga Sun, established in 1994, ceased publishing in July 2014 when they were merged to create The Sun.

title: Wasaga Sun
description: Metroland community newspapers The Stayner Sun, established in 1877, and  the Wasaga Sun, established in 1994, ceased publishing in July 2014 when they were merged to create The Sun.

title: The Sun
description: Metroland community newspapers The Stayner Sun, established in 1877, and the Wasaga Sun, established in 1994, merged to become The Sun in July 2014. On September 15, 2023, Metroland announced that all of its 71 community newspapers would immediately shift to digital-only publications, including The Sun. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists.

title: Almonte Gazette
description: The Almont Gazette ceased publication in 2009 after it merged with the Carleton Place Canadian to create the Carleton Place Almonte Canadian Gazette.

title: The Carleton Place Canadian
description: The Carleton Place Canadian ceased publication in February 2009 after it merged with the Almonte Gazette to create the Carleton Place Almonte Canadian Gazette.

title: Carleton Place EMC
description: Carleton Place EMC ceased to exist in 2011 when it merged with the  Carleton Place Almonte Canadian Gazette.

title: Carleton Place-Almonte Canadian Gazette
description: The Carleton Place Almonte Canadian Gazette was produced after a series of mergers that resulted in the demise of the Carleton Place Canadian and the Almonte Gazette in February 2009 and the Carleton Place EMC in 2011. On September 15, 2023, Metroland announced that all of its 71 community newspapers would immediately shift to digital-only publications, including the Carleton Place-Almonte Canadian Gazette. Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists.

title: Stittsville Richmond EMC
description: The Stittsville Richmond EMC ceased to exist after it was purchased by Metroland and merged with the Stittsville News in October 2011.

title: St. Thomas Times-Journal
description: The St. Thomas Times-Journal cut its Wednesday print and e-editions on January 31, 2023. The newspaper and e-edition will only be published on Tuesday, Thursday and Friday, while daily coverage continues online. The newspaper previously stopped publishing a Monday edition on December 22, 2008, when it was owned by Sun Media.

title: The Telegram
description:
After acquiring the newspaper from SaltWire Network earlier in the month, Postmedia announced on August 21, 2024, that the daily paper would become a weekly paper beginning with the August 30, 2024, edition. Four of the newspaperâ€™s 13 newsroom jobs were also eliminated. Daily news continues to be published online.
Previously, SaltWire Network had cut the Monday print editions of its daily newspapers, including The Telegram, effective October 17, 2022. Before that, the Sunday edition of The Telegram had been cancelled on September 21, 2008, when the newspaper was still owned by Transcontinental.

title: Brockville Recorder and Times
description: The Brockville Recorder and Times cut its Friday print and e-editions on June 17, 2022. The newspaper and e-edition will only be published on Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday, while daily coverage continues online. The newspaper previously stopped publishing its Wednesday edition on November 19, 2018, and its Monday edition in 2009.

title: Pembroke Daily Observer
description: The Pembroke Daily Observer published its final print edition on July 28, 2018. The daily newspaper had been publishing a print edition Tuesday through Saturday after previously cutting its Monday print edition. The Pembroke Daily Observer was founded in 1855. Postmediaâ€™s weekly newspaper, Pembroke News, and its website were renamed Pembroke Observer and News on August 2 to reflect the historical significance of the Pembroke Daily Observer.

title: L'Express Ottawa
description: The French-language weekly L'Express d'Ottawa ceased publication in the spring of 2015.

title: Westmount Examiner
description: After more than 80 years in print, the English-language weekly newspaper Westmount Examiner was closed by Transcontinental in October 2015.

title: The Yarmouth Vanguard
description: The Yarmouth Vanguard, a community newspaper, merged with The Digby County Courier and The Shelburne County Coast Guard to become a new print publication, The Tri-County Vanguard. The Tri-County Vanguard was first published Feb. 2, 2016. The Yarmouth Vanguardâ€™s website remains active and features stories about Yarmouth County.

title: The Digby County Courier
description: The Digby County Courier merged with the Yarmouth Vanguard and The Shelburne County Coast Guard to become The Tri-County Vanguard, which was first published Feb. 2, 2016. The Digby Courierâ€™s website remains active and features stories about Digby County.

title: The Shelburne County Coast Guard
description: The last print edition of The Shelburne County Coast Guard was distributed on Jan. 26, 2016 after 119 years of publishing. The community newspaper merged with the Yarmouth Vanguard and The Digby County Courier to become The Tri-County Vanguard, which was first published Feb. 2, 2016. The Shelburne County Coast Guardâ€™s website still remains active and features stories about Shelburne County.

title: Tri-County Vanguard
description: This weekly community print newspaper (published on Tuesdays) was the result of a merger between The Yarmouth Vanguard, The Digby County Courier and The Shelburne County Coast Guard. It was first published Feb. 2, 2016 and covers news for all three counties.

title: Ottawa Sun
description: Postmedia announced on September 21 that nine daily newspapers, including Ottawa Sun, would be cutting their Monday print editions effective October 17, 2022. Digital versions of the newspapers will remain available online. In early 2016, Postmedia announced the merger of the Ottawa Sun and the Ottawa Citizen newsrooms. Both newspapers continued to publish, but editorial staff work under one senior editor, filing stories and images to both publications.

title: Calgary Sun
description:
Postmedia announced on September 21 that nine daily newspapers, including the Calgary Sun, would be cutting their Monday print editions effective October 17, 2022. Digital versions of the newspapers will remain available online.
In early 2016, Postmedia, the country's largest newspaper chain, announced the merger of the Calgary Sun and the Calgary Herald newsrooms. Both newspapers continue to publish, but editorial staff now work under one senior editor, filing stories and images to both publications.

title: Calgary Herald
description: Postmedia announced on September 21 that nine daily newspapers, including Calgary Herald, would be cutting their Monday print editions effective October 17, 2022. Digital versions of the newspapers will remain available online. In early 2016, Postmedia, the country's largest newspaper chain, announced the merger of the Calgary Herald and the Calgary Sun newsrooms. Both newspapers continue to publish, but editorial staff now work under one senior editor, filing stories and images to both publications.

title: The West Island Chronicle
description: The long-running West Island Chronicle, an English-language weekly newspaper, was closed by Transcontinental in October 2015.

title: Guelph Mercury
description: The Guelph Mercury, one of the oldest newspapers in the country, published its last edition on Jan.29, 2016. All of the daily newspaper's 23 full-time staff and three part-time employees lost their jobs. The newspaper was founded in 1867.

title: Northern Journal
description: The Northern Journal weekly newspaper published its final print edition on March 2, 2016 and laid off its newsroom staff. The paper, which operated for 40 years out of Fort Smith on the border of the Northwest Territories and the Alberta border, covered the Northwest Territories as well as the Municipality of Wood Buffalo in northeastern Alberta, including Fort Chipewyan, Fort McMurray and the oil sands industry.

title: The Weekend Advisor
description: The Black Press-owned paper The Cariboo Adviser, the Williams Lake Tribune and its weekend edition, Tribune Weekend, merged on Sept. 4, 2013. They were replaced by the Weekend Advisor, published on Fridays, and the free community paper, the Williams Lake Tribune, distributed on Wednesdays. Black Press stopped publishing The Weekend Advisor (the Friday edition of the Williams Lake Tribune) due to the impacts of the pandemic. The final edition was published on March 20, 2020.

title: The Community Edition
description: The Community Edition, which started as a supplement to the WLU campus publication, The Cord, launched in 2012. But, with a quick growth in following, the publication evolved, dropping "The Cord" from its original name (The Cord Community Edition) and building its identity as a monthly covering arts, culture and social issues. Now known well across Waterloo Region, The Community Edition (TCE) is supported by one full time paid staff person and volunteer contributors. It's funded by a mix of advertising revenue and event revenue from a single, annual arts event called "Steel Rails."

title: Northeast News
description: This weekly newspaper closed Feb. 11, 2016. It had operated in the Peace Region since 2004.
Community: Dawson Creek and Fort St. John, British Columbia

title: Similkameen News Leader
description: The final issue of this independent newspaper was published July 26, 2016. The newspaper served the region for nearly 20 years.

title: North Shore Outlook
description: This weekly newspaper, which served north and west Vancouver, published its last edition on March 27, 2014.

title: Tillsonburg News
description: Postmedia merged Tillsonburg News, Lake Shore Shopper and Delhi News-Record to form Norfolk & Tillsonburg News on July 30, 2020.

title: Cranbrook Townsman
description: The Cranbrook Townsman reduced the publication frequency of its print edition from three times per week to once per week on April 7, 2020. The change was made due to the impacts of the pandemic. While the change was supposed to be temporary, the newspaper still remains a weekly publication. The newspaper had previously reduced the number of print editions in April 2016, shifting from a daily newspaper to a community newspaper that published three times per week (Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday). The newspaper was founded in 1946 in Cranbrook, BC.

title: Kimberley Bulletin
description: On April 7, 2020, the Kimberley Bulletin cut its Wednesday and Friday print editions and began publishing weekly on Tuesdays due to the impacts of the pandemic. While it was announced as a temporary change, the newspaper has remained a weekly publication. The Kimberley Bulletin launched as a daily newspaper in 1932 and reduced its publication frequency from five times per week (Monday through Friday) to three times per week (Tuesday, Wednesday & Friday) on April 19, 2016.

title: Burnaby News Leader
description: Closed six months after acquisition by Glacier Media from Black Press. Paper had published for 26 years.

title: Chetwynd Echo
description: After 57 years of reporting on the B.C. community of Chetwynd, the Chetwynd Echo community paper ceased publishing in January 2016.

title: Alberni Valley Times
description: The Alberni Valley Times published in the community for 48 years before closing Oct. 9, 2015. According to J-Source, the newspaper had been published daily until August 2015, when circulation was reduced to four times per week.

title: Tri-Cities Now
description: The community newspaper Tri-Cities Now served Coquitlam, Port Moody, Port Coquitlam, Anmore and Belcarra in British Columbia for over 30 years before publishing its final print issue on Oct. 1, 2015.
Community: Coquitlam, Port Coquitlam, Port Moody, British Columbia

title: Richmond Review
description: The Richmond Review published its final issue on July 24, 2015 after 83 years of circulation.

title: South Delta Leader
description: Glacier Media Group closed the South Delta Leader in 2014, along with multiple other British Columbia community newspapers.

title: Prince Rupert Daily News
description: Prince Rupert Daily News was nearing its 100th anniversary when it was closed by Black Press, which bought the daily newspaper from Glacier Media.

title: New Westminster  News Leader
description: The New Westminster Newsleader was one of two British Columbia community newspapers closed by Glacier Media on Oct. 1, 2015 soon after Glacier acquired the publication from Black Press

title: Elmira Independent
description: Established in 1974 and purchased by Metroland Media June 1998, The Independent failed to remain competitive. The corporate owner choose to attain profitability through several rounds of cost cutting. The editorial staff in 1998 consisted of 3 reporters and an editor. In 2015 just an editor and contributors.

title: Yorkton News Review
description: The Yorkton News Review, a weekly newspaper owned by Glacier Community Media in Saskatchewn, announced it would cease publication as of October 27, 2016.

title: The Independent
description: Originally a community newspaper, the Independent closed its doors in 2008. It was revived as an online news source in 2011.

title: Nanaimo Daily News
description: The Nanaimo Daily News ceased publishing on Jan. 29, 2016 after 141 years in business. Black Press bought the paper from Glacier Media in December 2014. The Black-owned  Nanaimo News Bulletin, which publishes twice a week, continues to operate.

title: Carstairs Courier
description: The Glacier Media-owned Carstairs Courier, a weekly community newspaper in Carstairs, Alberta, ceased publication after it merged with the Mountain View Gazette. The Gazette is distributed free of charge to the Alberta communities of Bowden, Torrington, Wimborne,  Crossfield, and now, Carstairs.

title: Maple Ridge-Pitt Meadows Times
description: The Black Press-owned  Maple Ridge-Pitt Meadows Times ceased publishing after it was merged with Maple Ridge-Pitt Meadows News.
Community: Maple Ridge, Haney, Pitt Meadows, British Columbia

title: Fort McMurray Today
description: On February 27, 2023, Postmedia shifted 12 community newspapers in Alberta to digital only publications, including Fort McMurray Today. On November 15, 2016, the newspaper reduced its print publication frequency to twice per week, down from six times per week.

title: Redvers Optimist
description: The Glacier Media-owned Redvers Optimist, serving the community of Redvers, Saskatchewan, ceased publication on Nov. 26, 2016.

title: Fort McMurray Connect
description: This print and online news organization shut shortly after the town's daily newspaper, Fort McMurray Today, announced it would only print thrice weekly and post more content online. In an interview with CBC, the Connect's editor cited a "tough market place" and the Fort McMurray wildfire in May 2016 as causes of the paper's closure.

title: The Free Press
description: The Free Press, a popular community newspaper that covered local politics in west-end Montreal, published its last issue at the end of February 2017. The free community newspaper, which was distributed twice monthly mostly through the mail, began publishing in 2009 after the closing of The Monitor, a longtime west end newspaper that had published for decades.

title: The Exponent
description: After publishing for 117 years, The Exponent, a family-owned weekly newspaper in Grandview, Manitoba, published its last edition on Feb. 28, 2017.

title: Rainy River Record
description: The Rainy River Record community newspaper, established in 1919,  ceased publication on Sept. 27, 2016 due to financial troubles.

title: Manotick News
description: Manotick News, a Metroland Media community newspaper, ceased publishing on Sept. 21, 2016,  when it merged with the Ottawa South News.

title: Town and Country
description: Town and Country, a community newspaper in Barrhead, Alberta, ceased publishing in June 2016 when it became the second section in the Barrhead Leader, Westlock News, Athabasca Advocate, and Lac La Biche Post, all of them owned by Great West Newspapers.

title: The Rocky Mountain Goat
description: The Rocky Mountain Goat announced on June 27, 2025, that the weekly newspaper will publish bi-weekly print editions for the foreseeable future. The Rocky Mountain Goat was founded as an independent weekly newspaper by Joseph Nusse and Laura Keil in 2010.
Community: Valemount, McBride and Robson Valley, British Columbia

title: The Jasper Local
description: The Jasper Local became a digital only publication on February 7, 2022. The Jasper Local launched as an independently-owned newspaper that published twice per month (and online) in Jasper, Alberta. The first issue was published on May 15, 2013.

title: Vegreville Observer
description: The Vegreville Observer, a privately owned weekly newspaper that served Vegreville, Alberta for 107 years, published its last issue on May 27, 2015.

title: What's Up Muskoka
description: PostMedia closed What's Up Muskoka, a community newspaper, on Jan. 6, 2016

title: Oxford Journal
description: After serving communities in Nova Scotia's Central and Eastern Cumberland County for 115 years, the family-owned Oxford Journal ceased publishing in March 2015.

title: North Bay Nipissing News
description: The North Bay Nipissing News, a community newspaper owned by Metroland Media Group, published its final edition on Oct. 8, 2015.

title: The Northern Pioneer
description: The Northern Pioneer community newspaper ceased publishing in April 2015 after it merged with the Echo, another community paper serving the community of High Level. Both papers were replaced by the Echo Pioneer.

title: The Echo
description: The Echo, a community newspaper established in 1973 to serve High Level, Alberta, ceased publishing in April 2015 after it merged with Fort Vermillion's Northern Pioneer. Both papers were replaced by the Echo Pioneer.

title: The Echo Pioneer
description: The Echo-Pioneer is a regional community weekly newspaper serving the municipalities of High Level and Mackenzie County, as well as providing regional news to Rainbow Lake, Paddle Prairie Metis Settlement, Dene Tha, Little Red River Cree, Tallcree and Beaver First Nations. The regional publication came into existence April 1, 2015, after the merger of two newspapers: The Echo, established in 1973, and The Northern Pioneer, established in 1976.

title: Valleyview Valley Views
description: After serving the Town of Valleyview, Alberta for 52 years, The Valley Views community newspaper published its last weekly edition in February 2015

title: The Georgian
description: The Georgian community newspaper, based in Stephenville, Quebec, was closed by Transcontinental Media in March 2014.

title: The Coaster
description: Transcontinental closed The Coaster, a community newspaper serving Harbour Breton, Nfld., in March 2014.

title: The Surrey Now
description: The Surrey Now community newspaper ceased publishing April 5, 2017 after Black Press merged it with the Surrey Leader to create The Surrey Now-Leader, which publishes every Wednesday and Friday. The newspaper launched in 1984.

title: The Surrey Leader
description: The Surrey Leader  community newspaper ceased publishing April 5, 2017 after Black Press merged it with Surrey Now to create The Surrey Now-Leader, which publishes every Wednesday and Friday. The newspaper was launched on July 17, 1929.

title: Surrey Now-Leader
description: Surrey Now-Leader became a weekly newspaper on March 26, 2020. While it was announced as a temporary change, the newspaper has remained a weekly publication. Black Press launched the Surrey Now-Leader as a community newspaper that published twice per week in April 2017 after closing The Surrey Now and The Surrey Leader.

title: North Delta Reporter
description: Black Press launched the North Delta Reporter on July 7, 2016. The weekly community newspaper serves the North Delta community in British Columbia.

title: Moose Jaw Times-Herald
description: The owner of the Moose-Jaw Times-Herald daily newspaper announced the daily would cease publishing as of Dec. 7, 2017 after more than 125 years in business. The paper had ceased publishing a Monday edition as of July 2016.

title: Kootenay News Advertiser
description: Black Press announced on March 14, 2017 that the Kootenay News Advertiser and Cranbrook Townsman would merge operations. The Kootenay News Advertiser no longer operates its own website, but has become a part of the Cranbrook Daily Townsman website. However, weekly print editions continue to be published. The newspaper began publishing weekly in April 2016, decreasing service from twice per week print editions.

title: Alaska Highway News
description: Alaska Highway News published its final edition on October 19, 2023. Glacier Media cited the changing media landscape and advertising shifting to online platforms as reasons why the newspaper was no longer sustainable. In March 2016, the paper reduced its publishing schedule, changing from a daily newspaper to a weekly newspaper. Alaska Highway News was founded in 1943.

title: Toronto Star
description: The Toronto Star launched a free tablet edition in September, 2015.

title: Manitouwadge Echo
description: The Manitouwadge Echo community newspaper ceased publishing in March 2016.

title: Maple Ridge-Pitt Meadows News
description: The Maple Ridge-Pitt Meadows News was consolidated with the Maple Ridge-Pitt Meadows Times by Black Press.

title: West End Phoenix
description: The non-profit West End Phoenix community newspaper serving Toronto's west end launched Oct. 13, 2017. The monthly paper is supported by subscriptions and donations from patrons.

title: Tumbler Ridge News
description: Tumbler Ridge News was established in 1997 and was distributed every Thursday for free. Publication ceased when the paper's owner became ill.

title: Chilliwack Times
description: The Chilliwack Times closed after 31 years of publication.

title: 100 Mile House Advisor
description: The 100 Mile House Advisor was purchased from Glacier Media by Black Press as part of the Cariboo Advisor Group in July 2010. Black Press closed the 100 Mile Advisor shortly after along with three other newspapers in the area.

title: Quesnel Advisor
description: Black Press bought the Quesnel Advisor from Glacier Media in 2010, along with 11 other B.C. community newspapers. The company closed four of these newly-purchased newspapers, including the Quesnel Advisor.

title: Brant News
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Frontenac Gazette
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Metro Ottawa
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Metro Winnipeg
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others. Metro Winnipeg published its first edition on April 4, 2011.

title: Nepean/Barrhaven News
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Stittsville News
description: This newspaper, which began in 1957, was swapped from Torstar to Postmedia in November 2017 along with two dozen other newspapers. Postmedia closed all but two of the papers it acquired during this transaction.

title: Ottawa South News
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Norfolk News
description: This was one of 24 newspapers Postmedia acquired from Torstar in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Postmedia kept just two of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Central Hastings Trent Hills News
description: This newspaper was swapped from Torstar to Postmedia in November 2017 along with two dozen other newspapers. Postmedia closed all but two of the papers it acquired during this transaction. The last issue of the Central Hastings Trent Hills News was published on November 23, 2017. Metroland merged Trent Hills Independent and Central Hastings News to form Central Hastings Trent Hills News on October 22, 2015.
Community: Central Hastings and Trent Hills, Ontario

title: Fort Erie Times
description: This was one of 17 newspapers Torstar acquired from Postmedia in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Torstar kept just four of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Innisfil Examiner
description: This was one of 17 newspapers Torstar acquired from Postmedia in a major newspaper swap in late 2017. Torstar kept just four of the papers open and closed the others.

title: Inport News

title: Niagara Advance

title: Mountain View Gazette
description:
Between the end of March and beginning of April, Great West Newspapers stopped publishing print editions of 10 community newspapers in Alberta and merged local content into three regional print and digital editions. Local content that previously appeared in print editions of the Athabasca Advocate, Barrhead Leader and Westlock News is now consolidated into the weekly print publication Town & Country This Week and appears on townandcountrytoday.com. Local content that previously appeared in print editions of the Bonnyville Nouvelle, Elk Point Review, Lac La Biche Post and St. Paul Journal is now consolidated into the weekly print publication Lakeland This Week and appears on lakelandtoday.ca. Local content that previously appeared in print editions of the Mountain View Albertan, Sundre Round Up and Innisfail Province (which has its own daily newsletter) is now consolidated into the weekly print publication The Albertan and appears on mountainviewtoday.ca. As a result, approximately 30 employees were laid off, representing about 15 per cent of the workforce. These layoffs include employees in sales, administration and 7 in news (proofreading, photographers, sports).
Mountain View Albertan was formed through the merger of the Mountain View Gazette and Olds Albertan. Before that, Mountain View Gazette merged with Didsbury Review and Carstairs Courier in 2016.

title: The Mississauga News

Nordstar put its Metroland Media Group division into creditor protection under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act as part of this restructuring plan that resulted in the loss of 605 jobs (approximately 60 per cent of its workforce), including 68 journalists. The newspaper was founded in 1965.

Torstar closed three Neighbourhood Voice newspapers on April 16, however staff will remain employed and work for the news website Toronto.com.

title: Regina Leader-Post
description: Regina Leader-Post, a daily newspaper in Regina, announced on June 4 that it will cease printing Monday editions effective June 22, 2020. Print editions continue to be published Tuesday through Saturday and an e-paper is available online on Mondays.

description:

title: Winnipeg Sun

title: Le Soleil
description:
CoopÃ©rative nationale de l'information indÃ©pendante (CN2i) announced on March 24, 2020, that its six daily French-language newspapers would temporarily limit their print publications to only Saturday weekend editions due to the impacts of COVID-19. On June 12, 2020, CN2i confirmed that the shift to digital weekday publication would be permanent, with only a Saturday print edition remaining.
The renamed Les Coops de l'information announced in March 2023 that all of its newspapers would become digital-only publications at the end of the year. Final paper editions for all six newspapers (Le Droit, Le Soleil, La Tribune, Le Nouvelliste, La Voix de l'Est, and Le Quotidien) were published on December 30, 2023.

description: Eckville Echo, a weekly community newspaper in Eckville, AB, published its final print edition on March 26, 2020. It was announced as a temporary change due to COVID-19. On June 2, 2020, the publisher confirmed that it would be permanent. Online local news coverage continues.

title: Yukon News
description: The community newspaper in Whitehorse, Yukon, that published three issues per week (Monday, Wednesday and Friday) decreased service to two issues per week in 2010. The newspaper no longer publishes a print edition on Mondays.

title: Melita New Era
description: Melita New Era, a community newspaper in Melita, Manitoba, announced on April 3 that it would temporarily suspend publication for 12 weeks starting on April 6, 2020. The newspaper permanently closed on September 3, 2020, "due to the uncertainty surrounding the economy in this time of COVID."

title: Reston Recorder
description: Reston Recorder, a community newspaper in Reston, Manitoba, announced on April 3 that it would temporarily suspend publication for 12 weeks starting on April 6, 2020. The newspaper permanently closed on September 3, 2020, "due to the uncertainty surrounding the economy in this time of COVID."

title: Souris Plaindealer
description: Souris Plaindealer, a community newspaper in Souris, Manitoba, announced on April 3 that it would temporarily suspend publication for 12 weeks starting on April 6, 2020. The newspaper permanently closed on September 3, 2020, "due to the uncertainty surrounding the economy in this time of COVID."

title: Deloraine Times & Star
description: Deloraine Times & Star, a community newspaper in Deloraine, Manitoba, announced on April 3 that it would temporarily suspend publication for 12 weeks starting on April 6, 2020. title: Vancouver Courier
description: Vancouver Courier temporarily stopped publishing a print edition, laid off staff, and reduced hours for remaining employees on April 2, 2020. The weekly newspaper permanently closed on September 10, 2020, and six employees were laid off.

title: Athabasca Advocate
description:
Between the end of March and beginning of April, Great West Newspapers stopped publishingÂ print editions of 10 community newspapers in Alberta and merged local content into three regional print and digital editions.Â 
Local content that previously appeared in print editions of the Athabasca Advocate, Barrhead Leader and Westlock News is now consolidated into the weekly print publication Town & Country This Week and appears on townandcountrytoday.com.Â 
Local content that previously appeared in print editions of the Bonnyville Nouvelle, Elk Point Review, Lac La Biche Post and St. Paul Journal is now consolidated into the weekly print publication Lakeland This Week and appears on lakelandtoday.ca.
Local content that previously appeared in print editions of the Mountain View Albertan, Sundre Round Up and Innisfail Province (which has its own daily newsletter) is now consolidated into the weekly print publication The Albertan and appears on mountainviewtoday.ca.
As a result, approximately 30 employees were laid off, representing about 15 per cent of the workforce. These layoffs include employees in sales, administration and 7 in news (proofreading, photographers, sports).

title: Langley Advance
description: Langley Advance published its final issue on February 21, 2019. The community paper merged with Langley Times to form the Langley Advance Times. The newspaper launched on July 23, 1931.

title: Langley Times
description: Langley Times published its final issue on February 27, 2019. The community paper merged with Langley Advance to form the Langley Advance Times. In early July 2009, the Langley Times dropped its publication schedule from three days per week to two. The newspaper launched in 1981.

title: Langley Advance Times
description: Langley Times and Langley Advance merged at the end of February, 2019, to form the Langley Advance Times. The new outlet published its first issue on March 1, 2019, and publishes a print edition every Wednesday and Friday.

title: Westman Journal
description: Westman Journal published its final issue on April 25, 2019. The free, weekly community newspaper serving Brandon, Manitoba, launched 17 years earlier as the Wheat City Journal on April 25, 2002.

title: The City Voice
description: The City Voice is a weekly community newspaper published on Tuesdays that covers local news for the greater Halifax Regional Municipality. The Dartmouth Tribune, Bedford-Sackville Observer and Halifax Citizen community papers merged to become The City Voice April 30, 2019. The paper was temporarily closed at the start of the pandemic when SaltWire announced on March 24 that all of their weekly publications would be suspended for 12 weeks. The last issue was published on March 24, 2020.

title: Halifax Citizen
description: Dartmouth Tribune, Bedford-Sackville Observer and Halifax Citizen community newspapers merged to become The City Voice on April 30, 2019.

title: Dartmouth Tribune
description: Dartmouth Tribune, Bedford-Sackville Observer and Halifax Citizen community newspapers merged to become The City Voice on April 30, 2019.

title: Bedford-Sackville Observer
description: Dartmouth Tribune, Bedford-Sackville Observer and Halifax Citizen community newspapers merged to become The City Voice on April 30, 2019.

title: Northeast Sun
description: Northeast Sun merged with Melfort Journal and Nipawin Journal to form The Journal on August 2, 2019. The last issue of the community newspaper was published on July 24, 2019.

title: Nipawin Journal
description: Nipawin Journal merged with Melfort Journal and Northeast Sun to form The Journal, a combined print publication, on August 2, 2019. The last issue of the community newspaper was published on July 24, 2019. Nipawin Journal continues to operate a website and social media sites.

title: Melfort Journal
description: Melfort Journal merged with Nipawin Journal and Northeast Sun to form The Journal, a combined print publication, on August 2, 2019. The last issue of the community newspaper was published on July 24, 2019. Melfort Journal continues to operate a website and social media sites.

title: The Journal
description: The Journal is a free, weekly print publication produced by the merger of Melfort Journal, Nipawin Journal, and Northeast Sun. The first issue was published on August 2, 2019.
Community: Melfort, Nipawin and surrounding area, Saskatchewan

title: Smoky River Express
description: Smokey River Express is a weekly community newspaper serving Falher, McLennan, Girouxville, Donnelly, Smoky River, and Peace River, Alberta. It launched on April 24, 2012. On September 11, 2019, Smokey River Express merged with South Peace News to form the print publication Smoky River Peace River Express. Smokey River Express continues to operate a website.
Community: Falher, McLennan, Girouxville, Donnelly, Smoky River, and Peace River, Alberta

title: South Peace News
description: South Peace News is a weekly community newspaper serving Triangle, High Prairie, Peavine, Gift Lake, Atkameg, Whitefish, Trout Lake, Grouard, Enilda, Kapâ€™Weno First Nation, Sucker Creek First Nation, Joussard, Driftpile First Nation, East Prairie, Faust and Kinuso, Alberta. It launched in 1962. On September 11, 2019, Smokey River Express merged with South Peace News to form the print publication Smoky River Peace River Express. South Peace News continues to operate a website.
Community: High Prairie and surrounding region, Alberta

title: Smoky River Peace River Express
description: Smoky River Express and South Peace News merged to form the weekly print publication Smoky River Peace River Express on September 11, 2019. The community paper covers High Prairie, Smokey River and surrounding regions.
Community: High Prairie, Smokey River and surrounding regions, Alberta

title: The Echo
description: The Echo, a bi-weekly community newspaper in Port Stanley, Ontario, launched on June 5, 2018. The newspaper closed the following year, publishing its last issue on July 2, 2019.

title: Carberry News-Express
description: Carberry News-Express is a weekly community newspaper that has been serving the Carberry community since 1910. The newspaper published its final issue on December 23, 2019.

title: Canadian Jewish News Toronto Edition
description: Canadian Jewish News relaunched as a news website with a national focus in January, 2021. Canadian Jewish News Toronto Edition, a weekly community newspaper serving the Toronto Jewish community, published its final issue on April 9, 2020. The closure was due to the economic impacts of COVID-19. Canadian Jewish News launched in Toronto in 1960, added a Montreal Edition in 1976, shut down temporarily in June, 2013, and was revived in early 2014.

title: Journal Pioneer
description: Journal Pioneer was a daily newspaper in Summerside, PEI, publishing Monday through Saturday. The newspaper launched as the Summerside Journal in 1865. On March 24, 2020, SaltWire Network suspended publication of the newspaper due to the impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic. Journal Pioneer returned as a weekly newspaper on November 4, 2020, publishing a print edition on Wednesdays.

title: La Nouvelle

title: Sherbrooke Express

title: Oxbow-Carnduff Herald-Gazette

title: Carnduff Gazette

title: Oxbow Herald
description: Carnduff Gazette and Oxbow Herald merged to form the Oxbow-Carnduff Herald-Gazette in January, 2017. The weekly community newspaper first published in 1913.

title: Saskatchewan Valley News

title: Cape Breton Community Post
description: Cape Breton Community Post, the free weekly sister publication of the Cape Breton Post, ceased publication during the week of February 1, 2017. Some of its content will be published in the Cape Breton Post. The newspaper launched in 2014.

title: Glengarry News

title: The Suburban
description: On April 19, 2023, The Suburban stopped door-to-door delivery service. The print edition of the newspaper was made available at public buildings and stores around Montreal instead. The change was in response to Mayor Valerie Planteâ€™s bylaw forbidding door-to-door delivery in Montreal.

title: Richmond News
description: Richmond News published its final print edition on November 30, 2023, becoming a digital-only publication. The weekly community newspaper in Richmond, BC, launched in 1977.

title: Fort Nelson News
description: Fort Nelson News, a weekly community newspaper in Fort Nelson serving the Northern Rockies, Alaska Highway and Liard Region, BC, published its final edition on September 13, 2023. The newspaper published its first issue on October 20, 1959.

title: Dawson Creek Mirror
description: Dawson Creek Mirror published its final edition on October 19, 2023. Glacier Media cited the changing media landscape and advertising shifting to online platforms as reasons why the community newspaper was no longer sustainable. The newspaper was founded in 1930 as the Peace River Block News.

title: Tilbury Times Reporter
description: Mohsin Abbas revived the Tilbury Times community newspaper, serving the town of Tilbury, ON, after it was closed by Postmedia in May 2020. The new Tilbury Times Reporter newspaper published its first issue on March 30, 2022.

title: Lakeshore News Reporter
description: Mohsin Abbas revived the Lakeshore News community newspaper, serving the Lakeshore region in Ontario, after it was closed by Postmedia in May 2020. The new Lakeshore News Reporter newspaper published its first issue on March 30, 2022.

title: LaSalle Post Reporter
description: Mohsin Abbas revived the LaSalle Post community newspaper, serving the town of LaSalle, ON, after it was closed by Postmedia in May 2020. The new LaSalle Post Reporter newspaper published its first issue on March 30, 2022.

title: the bridge
description: the bridge, a hyperlocal community newspaper serving the St. Lawrence, Moss Park, Corktown, Distillery District, Cabbagetown, Regent Park and Canary District neighbourhoods in Toronto, published its first edition in December 2019. The newspaper publishes a monthly print edition.
Community: St. Lawrence, Moss Park, Corktown, Distillery District, Cabbagetown, Regent Park and Canary District neighbourhoods, Toronto, Ontario

title: The Gleaner
description:
The Gleaner is a community newspaper that has served the Chateauguay Valley region in Quebec since 1863. The newspaper was locally-owned until 1985 when it was sold to media chain Les Hebdos MontÃ©rÃ©giens and later passed to Quebecor in 2011 and Transcontinental in 2013. The English-language Gleaner became an eight-page insert in the free French-language weekly Journal le Saint-FranÃ§ois in 2015 before being sold to GravitÃ© MÃ©dia in 2017 and closed in the fall of 2018. 
The rights to the paper were sold to Chateauguay Valley Community Information Services for $1 and the local community re-launched The Gleaner on June 5, 2019, as a monthly print edition produced by volunteers. It became a nonprofit organization in October 2019. The community newspaper began publishing bi-weekly print editions in January 2020.

title: The 1019 Report
description: The 1019 Report is an English-language community newspaper serving the Vaudreuil-Soulanges region in Quebec, including Saint-Lazare, Hudson, Vaudreuil and Rigaud. The newspaper published its first edition on May 13, 2020, and is only available in print, publishing bi-weekly on Wednesdays.

title: The Lunenburg Barnacle
description: The Lunenburg Barnacle, a once-a-month newspaper with a regularly updated website and active social media channels, launched February 9, 2023. It's a free, volunteer-run, non-profit publication serving Nova Scotia's Lunenburg County.

title: Le Saint-Armand
description: Le Saint-Armand published its final print edition on June 15, 2023. Weekly digital editions of the community newspaper continue to be published online. The newspaper launched in September 2003 and serves the Saint-Armand, Pike River, Bedford, Canton de Bedford, Notre-Dame-de-Stanbridge, Stanbridge Station, Stanbridge East, Saint-Ignace de Stanbridge, Dunham and Frelighsburg communities in Quebec.
Community: Saint-Armand, Pike River, Bedford, Canton de Bedford, Notre-Dame-de-Stanbridge, Stanbridge Station, Stanbridge East, Saint-Ignace de Stanbridge, Dunham and Frelighsburg, Quebec

title: Niagara-on-the-Lake Local
description: Niagara-on-the-Lake Local announced on February 17 that the weekly community newspaper would become an online only publication after the final print edition was published on February 28, 2024. The newspaper published its first issue on January 17, 2019.

title: Wreckhouse Weekly
description: Wreckhouse Weekly, a community newspaper providing local news coverage to the Port aux Basques community, announced in February 2024 that it would stop providing local news coverage and instead focus on publishing and printing business at Wreckhouse Press. The final edition of the newspaper was published on February 12. 2024. The weekly newspaper launched in August 2020 in response to the closing of The Gulf News a few months earlier due to the pandemic.
Community: Port aux Basques, Newfoundland and Labrador

title: Lakeland Regional
description: Lakeland Regional, a free weekly community newspaper serving the Lakeland region in Alberta, closed on April 3, 2018. The newspaper was a supplemental paper published by the Bonnyville Nouvelle. Great West Newspapers stopped publishing Lakeland Regional and the Bonnyville Nouvelle (previously a paid subscription paper) became the free community newspaper for the region.

title: The Yukon Star
description: The Yukon Star, a weekly community newspaper in Whitehorse, launched its website on May 22, 2024. The first print edition of the newspaper was published the following week on May 31. The newspaper launched in response to the closing of the Whitehorse Star a week earlier. The Yukon Star closed three months later due to financial difficulties, publishing its final edition on August 23, 2024.

title: Kawartha Lakes Weekly
description: The first edition of Kawartha Lakes Weekly was published on August 15, 2024. The weekly community newspaper serves the Kawartha Lakes community. The paper was launched in response to Metroland ceasing publication of Kawartha Lakes This Week a year earlier.

title: Kipling Clipper
description: Kipling Clipper published its first edition on September 17, 2021. The weekly community newspaper serves the Kipling, Windthorst, Kennedy and Langbank communities in Saskatchewan.
Community: Kipling, Windthorst, Kennedy and Langbank, Saskatchewan

title: Goderich Sun
description: Grant Haven Media launched the Goderich Sun, a free hyper local community newspaper serving the Goderich community, on September 1, 2023. The newspaper launched its first issue online and began publishing a print edition with its second issue on October 6, 2023. The newspaper became a bi-weekly publication on March 21, 2024.

title: Simcoe Advocate
description: The first issue of the Simcoe Advocate was published on September 26, 2024. The hyper local community newspaper serves the Simcoe community in Ontario.

title: Woodstock Ingersoll Echo
description: The first issue of the Woodstock Ingersoll Echo was published on June 16, 2023. The hyper local community newspaper serves the Woodstock and Ingersoll communities in Ontario. The newspaper became a bi-weekly publication on March 15, 2024.

title: Tillsonburg Post
description: The first issue of the Tillsonburg Post was published on September 19, 2024. The hyper local community newspaper serves the Tillsonburg community in Ontario.

title: Kamloops Chronicle
description: The Kamloops Chronicle published its first issue on October 25, 2024. The local community newspaper serves the Kamloops, BC, community and is published by the non-profit Kamloops Local News Society. The paper launched on the one-year anniversary of Kamloops This Weekâ€™s final edition.

title: Haida Gwaii News
description: Haida Gwaii News, a bi-weekly community newspaper serving the communities on the islands of Haida Gwaii in British Columbia, published its first issue on June 6, 2024.

title: The Grind
description: The Grind, an alternative magazine covering politics and culture news in Toronto, launched in October 2022. The website launched on October 1 and the first print edition was published on October 17, 2022. Print editions are published every two months and are available for free at over 300 locations around the Greater Toronto Area.

title: Smiths Falls Record-Journal
description: Smiths Falls Record-Journal, a weekly community newspaper serving Smiths Falls, ON, published its first issue on October 18, 2023.

title: Perth Courier-Journal
description: Perth Courier-Journal, a weekly community newspaper serving Perth, ON, published its first issue on April 3, 2024.

title: Estevan Mercury
description: The Mercury's editor announced on Facebook on January 8, 2025, that the 122-year-old paper was being closed "effective immediately" by its owners, Glacier Media and the Prairie News Group. The last edition of the community newspaper was published on January 1, 2025. The paper's online version had earlier been merged with Southeast Lifestyles in 2016, with a promise that the print editions would remain separate, however the print versions were also merged in 2020. The Mercury also operated the printing press that serves the Regina Leader-Post and the Saskatoon StarPhoenix, as well as several small community papers. The newspaper was founded in 1903.

title: Thompson Citizen
description: The final edition of the Thompson Citizen was published on December 20, 2024. The 65-year-old paper was purchased by Glacier in 2007. The weekly community newspaper served Thompson and northern Manitoba's regional hub and outlying communities throughout the North. The newspaper was founded in 1960.

title: Eagle Feather News
description: Eagle Feather News announced on February 12, 2024, that it was on hiatus due to a decline in advertising revenue following the implementation of the Online News Act. The free monthly newspaper had been distributed to Indigenous communities in Saskatchewan since its launch in the late 1990s. Through a partnership with Pattison Media, the local news outlet relaunched in July 2024 as a digital-first news platform with a quarterly print magazine renamed EFN Media. Pattison Media helped launch the redesigned website and will provide portal management and business support services, while Eagle Feather News will share content and provide access to its audience through an ad revenue and space sharing agreement.

title: Saskatchewan Indigenous News
description: Saskatchewan Indigenous News, a monthly community newspaper covering Indigenous news in Saskatchewan, published its first issue on April 1, 2024. It only publishes a print edition and does not operate a website.

title: Leaside Life
description: Leaside Life, a hyperlocal community newspaper serving the Leaside neighbourhood in Toronto, published its first issue on December 1, 2011.

title: Inuvik Drum
description: The final print edition of Inuvik Drum was published on January 9, 2025. The weekly community newspaper served the Beaufort Delta Region in the Northwest Territories. The local office in Inuvik was also closed. Online news coverage about the region will continue on NNSL.com, the regional website for all of the Northern News Services Limited newspapers, but there will no longer be a reporter located within the community. The first issue of Inuvik Drum was published on January 6, 1966.

title: Indian Time
description: The final edition of Indian Time was published on December 19, 2024. The weekly community newspaper served Akwesasne and sister Iroquois communities since its launch on July 1, 1983.

title: The Weekly Press
description: Advocate Printing & Publishing closed two of its weekly community newspapers, The Weekly Press and The Laker, on January 6, 2020. The Weekly Press covered local news in the Enfield, Nova Scotia, community.

title: The Laker
description: Advocate Printing & Publishing closed two of its weekly community newspapers, The Weekly Press and The Laker, on January 6, 2020. The Laker covered local news in the Fall River, Nova Scotia, community.

The website is no longer available and the last post on the outletâ€™s social media accounts was on April 22, 2022. The newspaper published daily digital papers as well as a weekly print edition. The newspaper launched in April 2017.

title: Herbert Herald
description: The Herbert Herald published its final edition on August 13, 2024 after 113 years of publication. The local community newspaper served the Central Butte, Chaplin, Ernfold, Herbert, Hodgeville, Morse, Mortlach, Parkbeg, and Riverhurst communities in Saskatchewan.
Community: Central Butte, Chaplin, Ernfold, Herbert, Hodgeville, Morse, Mortlach, Parkbeg, and Riverhurst, Saskatchewan

title: Rossland News
description: Rossland News, a community newspaper serving Rossland, British Columbia, published its final print edition on March 7, 2024. The website continues to provide daily local news coverage. Black Press Media announced on March 5, 2024, that the company would stop publishing a print edition of the paper. Local news coverage from Rossland News will instead be included in the West Kootenay Advertiser, which is an insert distributed inside the Trail Times, Castlegar News, Nelson Star, and Arrow Lakes News community newspapers.

title: The Express
description: The Express, a weekly community newspaper covering local news in Nelson, British Columbia, published its final edition on February 2, 2011. The publication had been serving the community since November 1988.

title: Nickel Belt News
description: The Nickel Belt News, a weekly community newspaper in Thompson, Manitoba, published its final edition on April 22, 2022. The newspaper was founded in 1961.

Beginning on June 2, 2021, the Manitoulin West Recorder became a part of the Manitoulin Expositor, appearing on page three of the weekly paper.

title: Algoma News Review
description: The final edition of the Algoma News Review was published on July 6, 2022. The community newspaper was founded in 1964 and served the  Algoma district in Ontario.

title: Stuart Nechako Advertiser
description: Stuart Nechako Advertiser published its final edition on July 11, 2024. The weekly community newspaper covered local news in Vanderhoof, Fort St. James, Fraser Lake, Fort Fraser, and the surrounding area in British Columbia. The newspaper launched in January 2006.
Community: Vanderhoof, Fort St. James, Fraser Lake, Fort Fraser, and surrounding area, British Columbia

The two papers had already shared an office and staff since around 2009. The first edition of the newspaper was published on October 21, 1980.

title: Rive-Sud Express.ca
description: The Rive-Sud Express.ca, a weekly community newspaper in Longueuil, has closed. The website was last updated on December 9, 2016. Les MÃ©dias de la Rive-Sud bought the newspaper in September 2014. Transcontinental launched the newspaperâ€™s website on April 1, 2010, and the first print edition was published on April 15, 2010.

title: East Central Alberta Review
description: The East Central Alberta Review published its final edition on March 27, 2025. The weekly community newspaper covered the Alberta communities between Kinsella to the North, Iddesleigh to the South, Torrington to the West and the Saskatchewan border to the East. The newspaper was founded in 1911.

description: Stratford Times became a weekly publication on August 14, 2025. On March 7, 2024, the newspaper increased its publication frequency, moving from twice-a-month (24 issues) to bi-weekly (26 issues). The free community newspaper serving Stratford, ON, was launched by Grant Haven Media as a monthly publication on August 12, 2021, and became a twice-a-month print publication in 2022.

Publication of the paper was paused after its May 29, 2024 edition while the company looked for a buyer. CHCO-TV, an independent broadcaster based in Saint Andrews, purchased the newspaper and revived it as part of the CHCO-TV website in November 2024. CHCO-TV began publishing a free monthly print edition of the newspaper on June 1, 2025.

title: Manotick Messenger
description: The Manotick Messenger closed as part of a transition to launch a new community newspaper (Manotick Messenger - Your Community Voice) that is part of the Your Community Voice chain of community newspapers in Ottawa. The last issue of the community newspaper was published on January 10, 2025. The community newspaper had been serving the Manotick community in Ottawa since 1987.

title: Manotick Messenger - Your Community Voice
description: The first issue of Manotick Messenger - Your Community Voice was published on January 23, 2025. The community newspaper was launched as part of a transition that included the closure of the Manotick Messenger newspaper to create a new newspaper that was part of the Your Community Voice chain of community newspapers in Ottawa. The newspaper is published every two weeks.

title: Barrhaven Independent - Your Community Voice
description: The first issue of Barrhaven Independent - Your Community Voice was published on January 23, 2025. The community newspaper was launched as part of a transition that included the closure of the Barrhaven Independent newspaper to create a new newspaper that was part of the Your Community Voice chain of community newspapers in Ottawa. The newspaper is published every two weeks.
