description: The Lambton-Middlesex Standard closed in October 2022. The weekly newspaper stopped publishing a print edition during the pandemic and had been distributing the newspaper as a pdf emailed to subscribers since then. After multiple mergers, the newspaper became known as the Lambton-Middlesex Standard in the mid-1990s.
Community: Lambton County and Middlesex County, Ontario

title: Lake of the Woods Enterprise
description: The Lake of the Woods Enterprise published its final edition on January 17, 2020. Postmedia merged the weekly community newspaper with the Kenora Miner & News, incorporating some of its columns and features into the Kenora Miner & News. The newspaper launched on December 1, 1996.

title: Jasper Fitzhugh
description: Jasper Fitzhugh announced on August 11, 2025, that the newspaper would be closing at the end of August. The loss of local businesses in Jasper due to the wildfires resulted in a reduction of ad revenue that made the newspaper unsustainable. The weekly community newspaper had been serving Jasper since 2005. It launched as an independent newspaper, was sold to Aberdeen Publishing in 2012, and has been published by Great West Media since 2023.

description: StÃ¨le MÃ©dias Inc ceased all commercial journalism activities on May 31, 2024. The final edition of Le Point d'Outremont was published on January 12, 2024. The newspaper was founded in 1998.

Community: St-FranÃ§ois and Duvernay neighbourhoods, Laval, Quebec

title: Powell River Peak
description: Powell River Peak is a community newspaper in Powell River that has served the qathet region in B.C. since it launched in November 1995. On May 1, 2025, the paper expanded its distribution by introducing newspaper drop boxes throughout the region. The newspaper had previously only been available from local stores or through home delivery for subscribers. On September 30, 2025, an independent owner purchased the newspaper from Glacier Media Group.

title: Arrow Lakes News
description: Arrow Lakes News, a community newspaper serving the Village of Nakusp in British Columbia, published its final print edition on July 17, 2025. The first edition of the newspaper was published on June 28, 1922.

title: Boundary Creek Times
description: Boundary Creek Times, a weekly community newspaper serving Greenwood, British Columbia, published its final print edition on March 31, 2022. The newspaper was founded in 1896.

title: Salmo Valley Newsletter
description: Salmo Valley Newsletter, a volunteer-run community newspaper serving the Village of Salmo in British Columbia, published its final edition on June 27, 2025. The newspaper was founded in 1983.

title: Ming Pao Toronto
description: Ming Pao announced on January 12, 2026, that the final editions of its Vancouver and Toronto newspapers would be published on January 16 and its Canadian newsrooms and offices would close on January 31. As a result, 60 employees across both newsrooms were laid off. The Chinese-language daily newspapers served the Vancouver and Toronto communities and launched in 1993.

title: Ming Pao Vancouver
description: Ming Pao announced on January 12, 2026, that the final editions of its Vancouver and Toronto newspapers would be published on January 16 and its Canadian newsrooms and offices would close on January 31. The Chinese-language daily newspapers served the Vancouver and Toronto communities and launched in 1993.

title: Prairie Dog
description: Prairie Dog closed on September 30, 2025, due to financial difficulties. The free biweekly alternative magazine launched in 1993 and served the Regina community.

title: Planet S Magazine
description: Planet S closed on September 30, 2025, due to financial difficulties. The free biweekly alternative magazine launched in 2002 and served the Saskatoon community.

title: The Carlyle Observer
description: The Carlyle Observer, a weekly community newspaper serving Carlyle, Saskatchewan, published its final edition on December 18, 2025. The newspaper was founded as the Carlyle Herald in the 1920s and became The Carlyle Observer in 1936.

title: Mackenzie Times

description: The Green Line is an independent local news website that publishes monthly issues covering local news in Toronto. The first issue was published on April 1, 2022.

description: Christopher Curtis in partnership with Ricochet Media launched the weekly newsletter The Rover on September 11, 2020. The newsletter covers local news in Montreal and Val-dâ€™Or.

description:
Six Rivers News, a local news website covering the Six Rivers to the Sea community in Nova Scotia, launched on April 1, 2016. Six Rivers News announced on June 29 that it would be closing and ceased all online news, radio and TV operations on July 1, 2024.

title: Burnaby NOW
description: Glacier Media announced on February 21, 2025 that Burnaby NOW, New Westminster Record, and Tri-City News would be closing. Glacier Media previously shifted Burnaby NOW, Tri-City News, and New Westminster Record from community newspapers to digital-only publications. Final print editions for all three newspapers were published on August 10, 2023.
